title: Angiopteris dianyuecola
taxonomy: Angiopteris dianyuecola is classified within the family Marattiaceae, which is a family of large, herbaceous plants commonly known as the mat ferns or marattioid ferns. This plant belongs to the genus Angiopteris, which is a group of large, tropical ferns native to Asia. The family Marattiaceae is part of the class Polypodiopsida, which includes all the ferns. Within the class Polypodiopsida, the plant is also part of the division Pteridophyta, which encompasses all vascular plants that reproduce via spores. The plant's classification is further refined by its position within the kingdom Plantae.

The plant is used in traditional medicine to provide various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, skin conditions, and respiratory problems.
